name: pyentrp

metadata.name: pyentrp
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: A Python library for computing entropy measures for time series analysis.

documentation:
# pyEntropy (pyEntrp)

![py39 status](https://img.shields.io/badge/python3.9-supported-green.svg)
![py310 status](https://img.shields.io/badge/python3.10-supported-green.svg)
![py311 status](https://img.shields.io/badge/python3.11-supported-green.svg)
![py312 status](https://img.shields.io/badge/python3.12-supported-green.svg)

1. [Quick start](#quick-start)
2. [Usage](#usage)
3. [Contributors and participation](#contributors-and-participation)

pyEntropy is a lightweight library built on top of NumPy
that provides functions for computing various types of entropy for time series analysis.

The library currently supports the following types of entropy computation:

+ Shannon Entropy ```shannon_entropy```
+ Sample Entropy ```sample_entropy```
+ Multiscale Entropy ```multiscale_entropy```
+ Composite Multiscale Entropy ```composite_multiscale_entropy```
+ Permutation Entropy ```permutation_entropy```
+ Multiscale Permutation Entropy ```multiscale_permutation_entropy```
+ Weighted Permutation Entropy ```weighted_permutation_entropy```

## Quick start

Install [pyEntropy](https://github.com/nikdon/pyEntropy) using pip:

```
pip install pyentrp
```

Install [pyEntropy](https://github.com/nikdon/pyEntropy) using poetry:

```
poetry add pyentrp
```

## Usage

```python
from pyentrp import entropy as ent
import numpy as np

ts = [1, 4, 5, 1, 7, 3, 1, 2, 5, 8, 9, 7, 3, 7, 9, 5, 4, 3]
std_ts = np.std(ts)
sample_entropy = ent.sample_entropy(ts, 4, 0.2 * std_ts)
```

## Contributors and participation

[pyEntropy](https://github.com/nikdon/pyEntropy) is an open-source project, and contributions are highly encouraged.
If you would like to contribute, you can:

- [Fork the repository](https://github.com/nikdon/pyEntropy/fork) and submit pull requests with your improvements, bug
  fixes, or new features.
- Report any issues or bugs you encounter on the [issue tracker](https://github.com/nikdon/pyEntropy/issues).
- Help improve the documentation by
  submitting [documentation improvements or corrections](https://github.com/nikdon/pyEntropy/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aopen+label%3Adocumentation).
- Participate in discussions and share your ideas.

The following contributors have made significant contributions to pyEntropy:

* [Nikolay Donets](https://github.com/nikdon)
* [Jakob Dreyer](https://github.com/jakobdreyer)
* [Raphael Vallat](https://github.com/raphaelvallat)
* [Christopher Schölzel](https://github.com/CSchoel)
* [Sam Dotson](https://github.com/samgdotson)

Contributions are very welcome, documentation improvements/corrections, bug reports, even feature requests :)

If you find [pyEntropy](https://github.com/nikdon/pyEntropy) useful, please consider giving it a star.

Your support is greatly appreciated!

functions.name: pyentrp.entropy.time_delay_embedding
documentation:
Calculate time-delayed embedding.

Parameters
----------
time_series : np.ndarray
    The input time series, shape (n_times)
embedding_dimension : int
    The embedding dimension (order).
delay : int
    The delay between embedded points.

Returns
-------
embedded : ndarray
    The embedded time series with shape (n_times - (order - 1) * delay, order).

functions.name: pyentrp.entropy.util_pattern_space
documentation:
Create a set of sequences with a given lag and dimension.

Parameters
----------
time_series : np.ndarray
    Vector or string of the sample data
lag : int
    Lag between the beginning of sequences
dim : int
    Dimension (number of patterns)

Returns
-------
pattern_space: np.ndarray
    2D array of vectors

Raises
------
ValueError: If the lag is less than 1 or the result matrix exceeds the size limit.

functions.name: pyentrp.entropy.util_granulate_time_series
documentation:
Extract coarse-grained time series.

Parameters
----------
time_series : np.ndarray
    Time series
scale : int
    Scale factor

Returns
-------
cts : np.ndarray
    Array of coarse-grained time series with a given scale factor

functions.name: pyentrp.entropy.shannon_entropy
documentation:
Calculate Shannon Entropy of the sample data.

Parameters
----------
time_series: np.ndarray | list[str]
    Vector or string of the sample data

Returns
-------
ent: float
    The Shannon Entropy as float value

functions.name: pyentrp.entropy.sample_entropy
documentation:
Calculate the sample entropy of degree m of a time_series.

This method uses Chebyshev norm.
It is quite fast for random data but can be slower is there is
structure in the input time series.

# Parameters

time_series

: Time series, 1-d vector

sample_length

: length of longest template vector

tolerance

: tolerance (defaults to 0.1 * std(time_series)))

# Returns

sampen: np.ndarray

: Array of Sample Entropies SE.
  SE[k] is the ratio `#templates of length k+1` / `#templates of length k`
  where `#templates of length 0` = n\*(n - 1) / 2, by definition

# Notes

The parameter 'sample_length' is equal to m + 1 in Ref[1].

# References

[^footnote-1]: <http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sample_Entropy>

[^footnote-2]: <http://physionet.incor.usp.br/physiotools/sampen/>

[^footnote-3]: Madalena Costa, Ary Goldberger, CK Peng. Multiscale entropy analysis of biological signals

functions.name: pyentrp.entropy.multiscale_entropy
documentation:
Calculate Multiscale Entropy considering different time-scales of the time series.

# Parameters

time_series

: Input time series for analysis.

sample_length

: Bandwidth or group of points

tolerance

: Tolerance value (default is 0.1 times the standard deviation of the `time_series`)

maxscale

: Maximum timescale (default is the length of the `time_series`)

# Returns

mse

: Array of Multiscale Entropies

# References

[^footnote-1]: <http://en.pudn.com/downloads149/sourcecode/math/detail646216_en.html>
    Can be viewed at <https://web.archive.org/web/20170207221539/http://en.pudn.com/downloads149/sourcecode/math/detail646216_en.html>

functions.name: pyentrp.entropy.permutation_entropy
documentation:
Calculate Permutation Entropy.

# Parameters

time_series

: Time series

order

: Order of permutation entropy

delay

: Time delay

normalize

: If True, divide by log2(factorial(m)) to normalize the entropy
  between 0 and 1. Otherwise, return the permutation entropy in bit.

# Returns

pe

: Permutation Entropy

# References

[^footnote-1]: Massimiliano Zanin et al. Permutation Entropy and Its Main
    Biomedical and Econophysics Applications: A Review.
    <http://www.mdpi.com/1099-4300/14/8/1553/pdf>

[^footnote-2]: Christoph Bandt and Bernd Pompe. Permutation entropy — a natural
    complexity measure for time series.
    <http://stubber.math-inf.uni-greifswald.de/pub/full/prep/2001/11.pdf>

# Notes

Last updated (Oct 2018) by Raphael Vallat (<mailto:raphaelvallat9@gmail.com>):
\- Major speed improvements
\- Use of base 2 instead of base e
\- Added normalization

# Examples

1. Permutation entropy with order 2

   > ```pycon
   > >>> x = [4, 7, 9, 10, 6, 11, 3]
   > >>> # Return a value between 0 and log2(factorial(order))
   > >>> print(permutation_entropy(x, order=2))
   >     0.918
   > ```

2. Normalized permutation entropy with order 3

   > ```pycon
   > >>> x = [4, 7, 9, 10, 6, 11, 3]
   > >>> # Return a value comprised between 0 and 1.
   > >>> print(permutation_entropy(x, order=3, normalize=True))
   >     0.589
   > ```

functions.name: pyentrp.entropy.multiscale_permutation_entropy
documentation:
Calculate the Multiscale Permutation Entropy.

# Parameters

time_series

: Time series for analysis

m

: Order of permutation entropy

delay

: Time delay

scale

: Scale factor

# Returns

mspe

: Array of Multiscale Permutation Entropies

# References

[^footnote-1]: Francesco Carlo Morabito et al. Multivariate Multi-Scale Permutation Entropy for
    Complexity Analysis of Alzheimer\`s Disease EEG. www.mdpi.com/1099-4300/14/7/1186

[^footnote-2]: <http://www.mathworks.com/matlabcentral/fileexchange/37288-multiscale-permutation-entropy-mpe/content/MPerm.m>

functions.name: pyentrp.entropy.weighted_permutation_entropy
documentation:
Calculate the weighted permutation entropy.

Weighted permutation entropy captures the information in the amplitude of a signal where
standard permutation entropy only measures the information in the ordinal pattern, "motif".

# Parameters

time_series

: Time series

order

: Order of permutation entropy

delay

: Time delay

normalize

: If True, divide by log2(factorial(m)) to normalize the entropy
  between 0 and 1. Otherwise, return the permutation entropy in bit.

# Returns

wpe

: Weighted Permutation Entropy

# References

[^footnote-1]: Bilal Fadlallah, Badong Chen, Andreas Keil, and José Príncipe
    Phys. Rev. E 87, 022911 - Published 20 February 2013

# Notes

- Updated in Jun 2023 by Nikolay Donets
- Updated in March 2021 by Samuel Dotson (<mailto:samgdotson@gmail.com>)

# Examples

1. Weighted permutation entropy with order 2

   > ```pycon
   > >>> x = [4, 7, 9, 10, 6, 11, 3]
   > >>> # Return a value between 0 and log2(factorial(order))
   > >>> print(permutation_entropy(x, order=2))
   >     0.912
   > ```

2. Normalized weighted permutation entropy with order 3

   > ```pycon
   > >>> x = [4, 7, 9, 10, 6, 11, 3]
   > >>> # Return a value comprised between 0 and 1.
   > >>> print(permutation_entropy(x, order=3, normalize=True))
   >     0.547
   > ```

functions.name: pyentrp.entropy.composite_multiscale_entropy
documentation:
Calculate Composite Multiscale Entropy.

# Parameters

time_series

: Time series for analysis

sample_length

: Number of sequential points of the time series

scale

: Scale factor

tolerance

: Tolerance (default = 0.1 * std(time_series))

# Returns

cmse

: Array of Composite Multiscale Entropies

# References

[^footnote-1]: Wu, Shuen-De, et al. "Time series analysis using
    composite multiscale entropy." Entropy 15.3 (2013): 1069-1084.
